title: ageratina tetragona
intro: Ageratina tetragona, also known as Mexican Mint Hill, is a plant species within the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to Mexico and Central America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Ageratina tetragona include relief from digestive issues, menstrual cramps, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Ageratina tetragona can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal uses, and it is also sometimes used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
